mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for memory management and request batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the vLLM CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the vLLM service.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.19.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with Google Cloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the vLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM with the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the vLLM release.
> Delete the cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size in your cluster configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a timeout in communication with the Kubernetes API.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure the Kubernetes API is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization techniques and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.18.0, review the new quantization settings in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud access before starting the installation.
